free_text: Double-differential neutron emission cross-section
measurements of 6Li and 7Li at incident neutron
energies of 8.17 and 10.27 MeV
Main reference
The HI-13 Tandem Accelerator
Deuterium gas target is 30*10 mm in length and
diameter, and gold backing is 0.5 mm in thickness.
Three main detectors ST-451 (10.16 x 5.08 cm)
and one monitor NE213 (2.54 x 2.54 cm) were used. The
energy resolutions for normal and abnormal case are
5% and 20%, respectively.
Four runs of measurement with gas-in
(sample-in and out) and gas-out (sample-in and out)
were performed for each angle during the experiment.
For each event, there are three parameters which are
PH, PSD and TOF. PH and PSD are used for the
detection threshold determination and n-gamma
discrimination. The contaminants of breakup source
neutrons from D(d,np) reactions was eliminated by
using the combination of abnormal and normal fast
neutron TOF spectrometers. The flight path between
sample and detector of normal and abnormal TOF
spectrometer is ~6 m and 70 cm, and the distance
between target and sample is 18 and 220 cm,
respectively.
Monte-Carlo simulated TOF spectra of the complete
angular distribution were iteratively adjusted to
the measured TOF spectra. The simulation comprised
the neutron production in the gas target and the
scattering geometry between target, sample and
detectors. The corrections due to the finite sample
size effects, flux attenuation and multiple scattering,
etc., were made with Monte Carlo simulation.
The uncertainties are mentioned as following
and their correlation.
Statistical uncertainty for normal and
abnormal measurement is 3-7% and 15%, respectively.
The uncertainty due to neutron
detection efficiency
The uncertainty due to correction for
multiple scattering
The uncertainty due to standard cross
sections
Data were
received from author
Entry was approved by author
Compiled by X4 group (CNDC).

VT. COMMON: ANG-ERR added
The lithium samples were encapsulated in aluminum
containers. The size of the containers is 30 X 40 mm
with 0.3 mm thickness of the wall. The composition of
sample is including 6Li, 7Li and Al as 90.3%, 8.6%
and 1.1% for the normal TOF spectrometer measurement,
respectively. The composition of sample is including
6Li and 7Li as 90.638% and 9.362% for the abnormal
TOF spectrometer measurement, respectively. And an
empty Al container was used to subtract background.
VT. COMMON: ANG-ERR added. ANG-CM->ANG and
DATA-CM->DATA (confirmed by Chen Guochang, 2026-04-30)
including
elastic and the 1st inelastic(Q=-0.478 MeV) levels
